skill.name: youtube-transcript-analysis-api-skill
skill.description: Extract YouTube video transcripts and perform deep competitive analysis on the content. Analyze video content strategy, competitor value propositions, target audience profiles, pain points, CTA strategies, content gaps, narrative structure, and emotional tone using an 8-dimension framework.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube transcripts via API and perform structured 8-dimension competitive content analysis.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All 86 static findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The skill makes legitimate API calls to browseract.com for YouTube transcript extraction and reads an API key from environment variables. The external_commands detections were triggered by markdown backtick syntax in documentation, not executable code. No malicious intent or security risks were identified.

low_findings.title: Third-party API dependency
low_findings.description: The skill requires a BrowserAct API key and makes network requests to api.browseract.com. Users must provide their own API key. This is a legitimate dependency for the stated functionality but represents a low risk factor.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear evidence of external API dependency with required user-provided API key. This is expected behavior for the skill but worth documenting as a low risk factor.

content.user_title: Analyze YouTube transcripts for competitive intelligence
content.value_statement: Manually watching hours of competitor videos is time-consuming and expensive. This skill extracts YouTube transcripts via API and performs structured 8-dimension analysis to reveal competitor messaging, audience targeting, and content gaps in minutes.
actual_capabilities: Extract transcripts from a single YouTube video URL using the BrowserAct API
Batch extract transcripts from multiple videos based on search keywords and date filters
Perform structured 8-dimension analysis including hooks, messaging, audience persona, pain points, viral drivers, credibility, business model, and content gaps
Generate cross-video comparative analysis for batch extractions
Output concise bullet-point reports in the language of the user's request
Handle API errors including invalid keys and concurrency limits with clear user guidance
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key provided by the user
Transcript quality depends on YouTube auto-generated captions which may contain errors
Analysis accuracy is limited when transcripts contain fewer than 50 words
API tasks may take several minutes due to automated browser operations

use_cases.title: Competitor video content strategy analysis
use_cases.description: Analyze a competitor's top-performing YouTube videos to understand their messaging framework, narrative structure, and call-to-action strategies. Extract insights to inform your own content creation.

use_cases.title: Batch niche research for product development
use_cases.description: Search for videos by keyword and analyze multiple competitor transcripts to identify recurring customer pain points, unmet needs, and industry content gaps for product ideation.

use_cases.title: Audience persona and messaging research
use_cases.description: Extract target audience signals, terminology level, and emotional triggers from competitor videos to build accurate buyer personas and refine marketing copy.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a single competitor video
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this YouTube video for competitive intelligence: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID. Focus on their hook strategy, core messaging, target audience, pain points, and CTA approach.

prompt_templates.title: Batch analyze videos in a niche
prompt_templates.prompt: Find and analyze the top 5 YouTube videos about 'AI automation tools' uploaded this month. Extract transcripts and perform the full 8-dimension analysis on each, then provide a cross-video comparative summary.

prompt_templates.title: Deep dive on viral content mechanics
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this viral YouTube video: URL. Focus heavily on Dimension 1 (Hook and narrative structure), Dimension 4 (Emotional arc), and Dimension 5 (Viral and engagement drivers). Explain why this video likely performed well.

prompt_templates.title: Content gap opportunity mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: Batch analyze 10 videos about 'SaaS marketing strategies' from this week. For each video, perform the 8-dimension analysis with special attention to Dimension 8 (Content Gaps). Then synthesize a master list of industry whitespace opportunities we could create content for.

output: ## Video Metadata
- Title: How to Automate Your Workflow with AI
- Channel: Productivity Pro
## Concise Deep Analysis
### 1. Content Structure & Hook
- Opening hook: 'What if you could save 10 hours every week?' — curiosity gap targeting time-strapped professionals
- Narrative framework: Problem-Agitate-Solve with before/after contrast
- Pacing: 15% intro hook, 70% core tutorial, 15% CTA
### 2. Core Messaging
- Single thesis: AI automation is accessible to non-technical users
- Supporting arguments: Personal story of wasting 20 hours/week on manual tasks
- Conclusion: Clear memorable takeaway — 'Automate once, save forever'
### 3. Audience Persona & Intent
- Target: Busy professionals and solopreneurs with beginner-to-intermediate tech skills
- Intent: Learn practical automation without coding knowledge
### 4. Pain Points & Emotional Arc
- Explicit pain: 'I was drowning in repetitive tasks every Monday morning'
- Emotional shift: From overwhelm and frustration to empowerment and control
### 5. Viral & Engagement Drivers
- Shareability: Relatable scenario of Monday dread; bold claim about 10-hour savings
- Memorable phrase: 'Your time is too expensive to spend on copy-paste work'
### 6. Evidence & Credibility
- Authority: Cites specific tool names and shows screen recordings
- Social proof: Mentions helping 500+ clients implement the same system
### 7. Business Model & Conversion
- Monetization: Lead generation for automation consulting services
- CTA: Free 15-minute automation audit with urgency via limited weekly slots
### 8. Categorized Content Gaps
- Creator weakness: No discussion of automation failure cases or risks
- Unresolved questions: How much does the recommended tool stack cost monthly?
- Industry whitespace: Security and privacy considerations for AI automation
### Key Takeaways
1. Lead with a quantified time-saving promise to capture attention immediately
2. Use personal before/after stories to make automation feel achievable
3. Address cost and security concerns that competitors omit to differentiate

output_examples.input: Batch analyze 'SaaS onboarding best practices' videos from this week
output: ## Cross-Video Comparative Analysis
- Common value propositions: Reducing churn through better onboarding, faster time-to-value
- Shared target audience: SaaS founders and product managers at early-stage companies
- Recurring pain points: Users abandoning during setup, low activation rates, support ticket overload
- Dominant content strategies: Case-study driven narratives with specific metric improvements
- Competitive differentiation: Some creators focus on email sequences, others on in-app tutorials, few cover both
- Industry content gaps: Onboarding for complex B2B SaaS with multiple stakeholders; localization strategies for global onboarding

best_practices: Always verify the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before running the script, and ask the user for it if missing
Do not output the full raw transcript text in responses; use it internally for analysis and only present structured metadata and insights
Keep the 8-dimension analysis extremely concise with bullet points and 1-2 short sentences per point to respect user time
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to scrape YouTube directly or bypass CAPTCHAs when the API is the intended extraction method
Do not fabricate analysis dimensions or invent quotes that do not appear in the actual transcript
Do not retry automatically when the error indicates an invalid API key or concurrency limit; guide the user instead

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a BrowserAct API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable, or provide it when the agent asks. You can get a key from the BrowserAct Console.

faq.question: How long does transcript extraction take?
faq.answer: Extraction typically takes a few minutes because it uses automated browser operations. The script outputs status logs with timestamps so you can monitor progress.

faq.question: Can I analyze videos in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. The transcript extraction works for any YouTube video with available captions. The analysis report will be written in the same language as your request.

faq.question: What is the difference between single and batch mode?
faq.answer: Single mode analyzes one video URL directly. Batch mode searches YouTube by keyword, filters by upload date, and analyzes multiple videos, then provides a cross-video comparative summary.

faq.question: Why should I not display the full transcript?
faq.answer: Transcripts are often very long and would clutter the response. The skill is designed to use the transcript internally for structured analysis and present only concise insights and metadata.

faq.question: What should I do if I see an 'Invalid authorization' error?
faq.answer: This means your BrowserAct API Key is invalid or expired. Do not retry. Go to the BrowserAct Console to verify or regenerate your API key, then provide the updated key.
